query: After ingesting the last source, what should happen next, and who should I hand this off to if I think there might be overlap with existing wiki content on the same topic?
expected_behavior: Recommends wiki-contradiction-detector as a follow-up when the newly ingested source covers a topic the wiki already discusses
Recommends a wiki-enhancer --coverage follow-up for dense sources that were only partially extracted in this pass
Does not claim wiki-ingest itself is responsible for cross-checking conflicts against existing pages — correctly states that's out of its scope
Correctly distinguishes its own scope (first-pass extraction from a source the wiki has never seen) from wiki-enhancer's scope (improving an already-ingested source)
